reasoning: The resolution dates 2025-11-09 and 2025-11-16 both fall on a Sunday, meaning the relevant market close prices are for the preceding Fridays: Nov 7 and Nov 14 respectively. Interpublic Group (IPG) was acquired by Omnicom (OMC) in a stock-for-stock merger that closed on Nov 26, 2025. Per the finalized merger terms, each IPG share was exchanged for 0.344 OMC shares. Because the merger was just two weeks away from closing during this window, IPG's stock price tracked OMC's stock price nearly perfectly. Historical data from Yahoo Finance shows OMC's price dropped from 72.82 on Nov 7 to 70.81 on Nov 14. Given the fixed 0.344 exchange ratio, IPG's stock price practically has to have mirrored this 2.7% decline. Therefore, the price on Nov 16 (Nov 14 close) was extremely likely to be significantly lower than the price on Nov 09 (Nov 7 close), resolving the question to NO.
